entries.prompt: A two-panel scientific illustration contrasting the outer-membrane architecture of a generic Gram-negative bacterium (left) versus Caulobacter crescentus delta-fur (right), side-by-side at equal scale, showing why lipid A is structurally essential in most species but dispensable in Caulobacter under this genetic condition. Left panel labeled 'Most Gram-negatives': a vertical cross-section of the Gram-negative cell envelope. The outer leaflet of the outer membrane is densely decorated with lipopolysaccharide (LPS) molecules whose anchor unit, lipid A, is shown as small orange hexagons packed tightly in the bilayer. The enzyme LpxC is indicated by a small labeled lock icon adjacent to the LPS layer ('LpxC — essential'). Inner membrane, peptidoglycan layer, and periplasm are shown below in muted gray-blue cross-section. Right panel labeled 'Caulobacter delta-fur': identical envelope cross-section in layout and scale, but the outer leaflet is now populated by ceramide phosphoglycerate (CPG) sphingolipid molecules rendered as elongated blue shapes instead of the orange lipid A hexagons. The LpxC icon is shown greyed-out with a strikethrough and labeled 'LpxC (absent)'. A small padlock icon labeled 'Fur (derepressed)' is shown open in the periplasm, indicating Fur regulation has been removed. Both panels share a clean vertical layering: outer membrane (top), periplasm with peptidoglycan mesh (middle), inner membrane (bottom), with structural elements color-coded consistently — lipid A in #F78E1E orange, CPG sphingolipid in #007DC3 blue, peptidoglycan in #5E9732 green, membranes in light gray. Style: clean scientific illustration in the style of a textbook figure or Nature publication graphical abstract — flat colors, thin black outlines, professional academic aesthetic. Use KBase brand palette: #007DC3 (blue), #5E9732 (green), #F78E1E (orange). White background. Aspect ratio 16:9. Text labels in clean sans-serif: 'Most Gram-negatives', 'Caulobacter delta-fur', 'Lipid A (LPS anchor)', 'LpxC (essential)', 'LpxC (absent)', 'CPG sphingolipid', 'Fur (derepressed)', 'Outer membrane', 'Peptidoglycan', 'Inner membrane'. Do not include any other text, captions, titles, or annotations.

A three-panel scientific illustration framing the three open research questions of the Δfur–ΔlpxC lipid-A-loss rescue in Caulobacter crescentus. Style: clean scientific illustration in the style of a textbook figure or Nature publication graphical abstract — flat colors, thin black outlines, professional academic aesthetic. Use the KBase brand palette: #007DC3 (blue), #5E9732 (green), #F78E1E (orange). White background. Aspect ratio 16:9. Three equal-width panels separated by thin vertical rules.

Panel 1 (left, #007DC3 blue): A schematic Fur repressor protein drawn as a circle labeled 'Fur', shown removed with a dashed outline and a diagonal strikethrough, with a branching fan of short outward arrows radiating from the deletion site. The fan is labeled '93 Fur-released DEGs'. A stylized question mark sits at the outer edge of the fan, with the text label 'Which are required?' beneath it. All arrows in #007DC3 on a white background.

Panel 2 (center, #5E9732 green): A cross-sectional cartoon of the Caulobacter outer membrane. The outer leaflet shows a dashed rectangular space labeled 'ΔlpxC' (indicating lipid A absent) filled by small ceramide phosphoglycerate icon shapes (two-tailed lipid symbols) labeled 'CPG'. A horizontal transport arrow labeled 'Lpt' points from an inner-membrane baseline toward the outer leaflet. A question mark above the outer leaflet is labeled 'CPG + Lpt sufficient?'. Lipid icons and arrows in #5E9732.

Panel 3 (right, #F78E1E orange): Four bacterial cell silhouettes arranged in a 2×2 grid. Top-left: Caulobacter crescentus silhouette labeled 'Caulobacter' with a filled checkmark circle. Top-right, bottom-left, bottom-right: silhouettes of A. baumannii, N. meningitidis, and M. catarrhalis, each labeled by species name and bearing an X circle. A question mark above the 2×2 grid is labeled 'Why only Caulobacter?'. Cell outlines, labels, and icons in #F78E1E.

Text labels in clean sans-serif placed immediately adjacent to their elements: 'Fur', '93 Fur-released DEGs', 'ΔlpxC', 'CPG', 'Lpt', 'Caulobacter', 'A. baumannii', 'N. meningitidis', 'M. catarrhalis', 'Which are required?', 'CPG + Lpt sufficient?', 'Why only Caulobacter?'. Do not include any other text, captions, titles, or annotations.

Three-panel scientific infographic showing three converging evidence streams of the Caulobacter lipid A-loss study. Overall layout: three equal vertical panels arranged side-by-side, each with a colored header band, with convergent arrows from all three panels meeting at a single centered outcome label at far right reading 'Δfur → envelope rescue'. Panel 1 (blue header band, #007DC3, header label 'Fur-signal isolation'): a horizontal strain-series schematic depicting three bacterial cell outlines in a left-to-right row, labeled '4580', '4584', '4599' respectively, with right-pointing arrows between them; the outermost membrane ring of cell '4599' is visually absent to indicate lipid A loss. A dashed crosslink arrow from an external rectangle labeled 'Leaden 2018 Δfur RNA-seq' enters the diagram at the 4580→4584 comparison step, indicating cross-referencing. Panel 2 (green header band, #5E9732, header label 'Regulon triage'): a narrowing funnel schematic. At the top, an input rectangle labeled '198 RB-TnSeq experiments'. The funnel narrows downward to a central block labeled '32 Fur-released genes'. Below the funnel, a horizontal calibration bar labeled '33.25% genome background'. A short exit arrow points right, labeled 'fitness-enriched'. Panel 3 (orange header band, #F78E1E, header label 'Envelope characterization'): a vertical stack of three labeled rows, each with a small icon, all feeding a shared right-pointing exit arrow: top row labeled 'transcript DE', middle row labeled 'OM proteome', bottom row labeled '4-species annotation'. Style: clean scientific infographic in the style of a Nature Methods graphical abstract — flat colors, thin black outlines, professional academic aesthetic. KBase brand palette as specified above for panel headers. White background. Aspect ratio 16:9. All text in clean sans-serif as specified. Do not include any other text, captions, titles, decorative borders, or annotations beyond the named labels.
